rationale: The cited paper is used as more than background: it is explicitly invoked to support the mechanism-level distinction between compromise choices (more effortful trade-off processing) and attraction effects (more perceptual or intuitive processing). Those claims are central premises for the citing paper's hypotheses about how depletion should shift context effects. The citation also reappears in framing and discussion, where results are interpreted against this prior process account.
relation_description: The citing paper treats Dhar and Simonson (2003) as a conceptual foundation for mechanism claims about context effects, then tests a new antecedent (resource depletion) predicted to alter those effects. In this way, it builds its core hypothesis logic on the cited paper's account of compromise versus attraction processing.
